inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; soil surface appears barren.

plant_audit.notes: Across the entire chronological sequence, there is zero evidence of active, healthy, or even decaying plant matter. The pots contain only soil and debris (eggshell fragments).

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and appears moist, but lacks any vegetative cover.
biome_observations.debris: Persistent presence of white, jagged fragments (likely crushed eggshells) in the foreground pot.
biome_observations.anomalies: The 'White Rabbit' anchor is present in the foreground pot but is not accompanied by any Pothos foliage.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_Current: Static. No growth, no movement, no change in soil composition or plant presence detected over the 5-day observation window.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There are no living specimens to assess. The environment appears to be a 'ghost' setup where the containers remain but the biological components have been removed or have perished completely.
anomalies: Presence of non-organic debris (eggshells) in P3.
Total absence of expected plant life across all registered pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first action was to establish the baseline of the pots and their expected contents. Upon validation, it is clear that the registry does not match the physical reality. The pots are empty of plant life. The sequence shows no change over time, indicating a static, non-productive environment. The presence of the white rabbit anchor in an empty pot confirms that the lack of plant life is not a camera angle issue, but a systemic loss of the botanical specimens.
